    Check renv.lock packages for Windows / OS compatibility

    check_renv_packages.Rd

    Parses the project's renv.lock and tests each package against three criteria:

    1. CRAN packages with OS_type: unix (will not install on Windows).

    2. CRAN packages that lack a Windows binary (require compilation with Rtools).

    3. GitHub-sourced packages that declare NeedsCompilation: yes (require Rtools on Windows).

    check_renv_packages(
  lock_path = file.path(getwd(), "renv.lock"),
  cran_mirror = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"
)

    Arguments

    lock_path

    Character. Path to the renv.lock file. Defaults to "renv.lock" in the current working directory.

    cran_mirror

    Character. CRAN mirror URL.

    Value

    A named list with three data frames:

    unix_only

    CRAN packages with OS_type: unix.

    no_win_binary

    CRAN packages with no Windows binary.

    github_needs_compile

    GitHub packages requiring compilation.

    Each data frame contains at minimum a package column.

    Details

    Network access is required for checks 1, 2, and 3. Tests are skipped gracefully if the CRAN database cannot be fetched.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
res <- check_renv_packages(here::here("renv.lock"))
res$unix_only
res$no_win_binary
} # }

    Contents
